Subject: Last-Minute Debt Deal
Body: Forbes NewslettersPlus: How AmericaвАЩs Property Barons Will Survive The Commercial Real Estate CrisisADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER вАҐ Newsletters вАҐ MyForbesGood morning, If you were on a news break over the long Memorial Day weekend, we have you covered. Here's all the latest on the debt ceiling deal. The Miami Heat and Denver NuggetsвАФand their billionaire ownersвАФare advancing to the NBA finals, and the live-action remake of The Little Mermaid is No. 1 at the box office. There's much more, including Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger's advice on buying good companies in tough times. Have a great four-day workweek, Caroline Howard Executive Editor Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSGETTY IMAGESPresident Joe Biden expressed confidence that a bill to avert a debt ceiling crisis will pass by the June 5 deadline to prevent default, although some lawmakers are threatening to derail the bipartisan legislation that was unveiled after a last-minute negotiating marathon between the White House and GOP congressional leaders. We have the details: Flat spending for fiscal year 2024 and an increase by 1% for 2025; raising the age for work requirements for government aid such as SNAP benefits; fully funded medical care for veterans; a compromise on the federal permitting process for energy projects; and the withdrawal of unused Covid-19 relief funds from past Congressional bills.Federal Student Loan Repayments: The Biden administrationвАЩs pause will end in late August, although the fate of the White HouseвАЩs student loan waiver program has been left to the Supreme Court.TECH + INNOVATIONDisenchanted with social media options like Twitter, the reclusive Bitcoin programmer @Fiatjaf tells Forbes how he designed his chat-and-payment service for people who dislike corporate control. Meet the mysterious Nostr creator who gained 18 million users and $5 million from Jack Dorsey.With a market cap nearing $1 trillion and a stock price that has surged more than 170% since the start of this year, NvidiaвАЩs investors are excited about the chip makerвАЩs futureвАФa sentiment the companyвАЩs oldest and most vocal consumers, gamers, donвАЩt appear to share. Gamer anger stems mainly from what they say are steep prices, confusing marketing, lack of features and lackluster performance in the RTX 4000 series.WEALTH + ENTREPRENEURSHIPLet's tune into two billionaires whose optimism has made them exceptionally wealthy. Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger offer keen intellect, deep wisdom and omniscience of the current business and investment world, delving into discussions about the slowing economy and the impact of AI. "I am personally skeptical of some of the hype that's gotten into artificial intelligence," says Munger. "I think old-fashioned intelligence works pretty well."MONEY + POLITICSTexas legislators have sent a bill to Republican Gov. Greg Abbott that would ban diversity, equity and inclusion efforts at public universities, just weeks after Florida Gov. Ron signed a similar bill into law. The bill seeks to prohibit public college DEI offices, programs and trainingsвАФand would require all hiring practices be вАЬcolor-blind and sex-neutral.вАЭSCIENCE + HEALTHCAREGETTY IMAGESA probable fungal meningitis outbreakвАФan infection that causes inflammation near the brain and spinal cordвАФin Mexican medical clinics has left two Americans dead after cosmetic surgeries and over 200 infected, causing the U.S. and Mexico to ask the World Health Organization to issue a public health emergency declaration.A beluga whale that became famous in 2019 after it was suspected of being trained by the Russian military re-emerged off Sweden's southwestern coast, leading an organization tracking him to work with authorities to ensure the whale is safe.SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTGETTY IMAGESThe Miami Heat staved off a nearly historic comeback by the Boston Celtics last night, advancing to the NBA Finals in a thrilling seven-game Conference FinalsвАФsetting up a Finals match-up between two billionaire-owned teams more than a week after the Denver Nuggets swept the Los Angeles Lakers.The 76th Cannes Film Festival concluded this weekend with the French film directed by Justine Triet, Anatomy of a Fall, awarded the top prize, while no American film entries received any honors. Martin ScorseseвАФwho won the Palme dвАЩOr in 1975 for Taxi DriverвАФdebuted his film Killers of the Flower Moon, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, at the festival but was screened outside of competition.Daily Cover Story GETTY IMAGESStay Alive Until 2025: How AmericaвАЩs Property Barons Plan To Survive The Commercial Real Estate CrisisRead ArticleTOPLINEAs millions of Americans stayed home to avoid Covid-19 in 2020, one of the most iconic buildings in the nationвАЩs capital, Union Station, sat virtually empty. The Beaux ArtsвАУstyle rail terminal with offices and retail space was one of the jewels in the real estate portfolio of billionaire Ben Ashkenazy (net worth: $2.6 billion), who purchased an 84-year lease on the property for $160 million in February 2007.The lack of passengers quickly took its toll. In May 2020, Ashkenazy defaulted on $430 million in loans on the station that he had refinanced in 2018, according to court records in a foreclosure lawsuit filed against him by the lender, New YorkвАУbased Rexmark. Seven months later, Ashkenazy lined up a deal with an unnamed sovereign wealth fund to take a 50% stake in the terminal for more than $700 million.But that deal collapsed in 2021вАФand in another twist, Amtrak moved to take over Union Station for $250 million via eminent domain in April 2022. Now the landmark is at the heart of a legal battle between the federal government, Ashkenazy and Rexmark, which maintains that it purchased Union Station for $140.5 million at a foreclosure auction last June.One thing is certain: The commuters arenвАЩt coming back. AmtrakвАЩs annual passenger numbers decreased from more than 5 million in 2019 to 1.8 million in 2021, a 66% drop. Ridership on two key routes through Union Station dropped 26% to 9.2 million through 2022.WHY IT MATTERSThe plight of Union Station is one of the most visible manifestations of the crisis facing AmericaвАЩs urban cores, which for dec¬≠ades relied on a steady stream of office workers to remain economically vibrant. More than three years after the lockdowns, itвАЩs obvious that those workers are never returning in the same numbers. Daily commuters are down an average of 39% across six markets. As a result, office towers are languishing, losing nearly 20 million square feet of leases in the first quarter of 2023. A record 962 million square feet of office space is now vacant in the U.S.вАФ20.2% of the countryвАЩs entire stockвАФwith the highest vacancies in New York, Washington, D.C., and Chicago.MOREInside King Charles IIIвАЩs $25 Billion Real Estate EmpireADVERTISEMENTFACTS AND COMMENTSThe Little Mermaid, the highly-anticipated live-action Disney remake, earned the No. 1 spot at the box office this weekend, drawing $118 million in domestic sales in its first weekend in theaters. The film brought in an ethnically diverse audience of ticket-buyers.35%Black 33%White 23%Latino9%AsianSTRATEGY AND SUCCESSIf you sense an unsettling erosion of the emotional connection and closeness in your relationship, it may indicate that your partner is вАЬquiet dumpingвАЭ or вАЬquiet quittingвАЭ your relationship. It can be challenging to identify the subtleties. Here are five tell-tale signs, according to a psychologist. What is the best-selling video game of all time?A.MinecraftB.TetrisC.Mario Kart 8 DeluxeD.Grand Theft Auto VCheck Your AnswerFRASE BY FORBESUnscramble todayвАЩs anagram to solve this hidden phrase!Scroll to the bottom of todayвАЩs newsletter for a hint.Play Now TODAY'S PHRASE ACROSS THE NEWSROOMAnnouncing the 2023 Forbes 30 Under 30 Local Lists. We're looking for the top young founders and leaders in the innovation hubs of Atlanta, Austin, Boston, Chicago, Cleveland, Los Angeles, Miami, Puerto Rico, Seattle and Toronto. The deadline to submit nominations is June 9.The Forbes third annual 50 Over 50 is officially looking for its next class of women proving that success has no age limit. WeвАЩre looking for women stepping into their own after age 50. Submit your nomination today. BEYOND THE NEWSROOMThe 2023 Forbes Future Of Work Summit will take place in New York and virtually on June 1. WeвАЩll explore the forthcoming impact of artificial intelligence, shifting dynamics between workers and employers, and more. Sign up to stay tuned for updates.The 2023 Forbes Iconoclast Summit will convene the world's most iconic visionaries, financial titans, pioneering technologists and key executivesвАФincluding Mellody Hobson and Ray DalioвАФfor conversations on the most critical issues impacting our economy and markets today. Join us in New York City on June 12.Thanks for reading! Follow us on Twitter for by-the-minute updates on the latest business and financial news throughout the day. Subject: Re: [opensuse] Memory leakage, or normal? Was: Huge amount usage of ram memory ( i have 2Gb and free 121 Mb )
Body: On Sunday 10 February 2008 15:30:51 Tom Patton wrote: > But...if it still remained in memory when the time came for Beagle to > index that new batch of email, that would be a good thing... What you're talking about is the page cache, which is shared among programs. But that wouldn't show up as an increase in RSS for kmail > And for the "green crowd" Don't do this, not on a technical mailing list. Anders ' -- Madness takes its toll

Subject: Your balance has dropped below zero пШ≠
Body: Your balance is below zero Add money to use your card Add money Hi Anonzmous, Your HUF balance has dropped below zero. To start using your card again, and to avoid our recovery process, just top up your account within 7 days and bring your balance back above zero. There are a bunch of quick and easy ways you can top up: Bank Transfer пП¶ Top up from an external bank account in your local currency. You can see the full details of topping up by Bank Transfer here. Card пТ≥ Link your bank card to your Revolut account for one of the fastest and easiest ways to add money. Successful top-ups will appear instantly in your wallet! Get a breakdown of topping up by card here. Apple Pay and Google Pay пУ± Just tap вАШTop upвАЩ in-app and select Apple or Google Pay from the drop-down menu. You can even top up from your Apple Watch by opening the Revolut app and hitting the button at the bottom of the balances screen. Auto Top-up пТ∞ Go to вАШTop upвАЩ in the app, select your card, and tap 'Set up auto top up'. Then choose the conditions of your auto top up by selecting the amount you wish to top up when your balance falls below an amount you set. Get the full details here. Looking for more info? You can find out more about our negative balance policy in our Terms and Conditions. If you have any questions about this, or if you are experiencing financial difficulties that impact your ability to bring your balance above zero, please contact our in-app support вАУ we're happy to help. Thanks, Team Revolut Add money ¬© 2024 Revolut Bank UAB If you would like to find out more about which Revolut entity you receive services from please click here. Subject: Update of LC Science Tracer Bullet: Wind Power
Body: Update of LC Science Tracer Bullet: Wind Power [ http://www.loc.gov/rr/scitech/tracer-bullets/windpowertb.html ] Wind power or wind energy is the process by which the wind is used to generate mechanical power or electricity and is one of the fastest-growing forms of electricity generation in the world. An update of TB 81-5, this guide is a review of the literature in the Library's collections on wind power with an emphasis on recent material. ________________________________________________________________________ Update your subscriptions, modify your password or e-mail address, or stop subscriptions at any time on your Subscriber Preferences Page [ https://service.govdelivery.com/service/user.html?code=USLOC ]. Subject: Re: Question regarding timeouts.
Body: Justin Piszcz wrote: > Question-- > > 1. Are these typical to see in the logs? > 2. Some are caused by using fail2ban of course, others by spammers. > > $ grep 'timeout after' mail.log |\\ > sed 's/from/|/g' |\\ > awk '{print "timeout after",$8,$10}' |\\ > awk '{print $1,$2,$3}'|sort|uniq -c > 549 timeout after CONNECT > 613 timeout after DATA > 419 timeout after EHLO > 75 timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > 137 timeout after HELO > 160 timeout after MAIL > 821 timeout after RCPT > 294 timeout after RSET > > 3. However, there are also some legitimate timeouts: > > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after EHLO vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > qmta01.westchester.pa.mail.comcast.net[76.96.62.16] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > qmta02.emeryville.ca.mail.comcast.net[76.96.30.24] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > qmta09.westchester.pa.mail.comcast.net[76.96.62.96] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > are you doing heavy header and body checks? header and body checks should not be abused: these are not a generic content filter. > -- > > For awhile, I have had the following enabled (now using the defaults): > > #smtpd_client_connection_count_limit = 25 > #smtpd_client_connection_rate_limit = 50 > #smtpd_error_sleep_time = 10 > #smtpd_hard_error_limit = 5 > #smtpd_junk_command_limit = 3 > #smtpd_soft_error_limit = 2 > #smtpd_timeout = 60s > > These were mainly extracted from: > http://www.pantz.org/software/postfix/setuppostfix.html > > After replacing these with the defaults: > > 1 200-126-83-1.bk6-dsl.surnet.cl[200.126.83.1] > 1 82.158.130.242.dyn.user.ono.com[82.158.130.242] > 1 84.121.208.253.dyn.user.ono.com[84.121.208.253] > 1 adsl-ull-125-230.49-151.net24.it[151.49.230.125] > 1 gwfry2-1-190.802.cz[85.132.248.190] > 1 pool-72-77-86-211.pitbpa.fios.verizon.net[72.77.86.211] > 2 172-143-89-200.fibertel.com.ar[200.89.143.172] > 2 h222.144.16.98.ip.alltel.net[98.16.144.222] > 3 fl-71-52-8-35.dhcp.embarqhsd.net[71.52.8.35] > 4 ppp340D.dsl.pacific.net.au[203.143.213.13] > 9 88-199-92-2.tktelekom.pl[88.199.92.2] > 9 n24z185l52.broadband.ctm.net[202.86.185.52] > > I still get various timeout after (X) but as you can tell, they are > most likely all spam/zombie hosts (most likely blocked via > iptables/fail2ban). No, they probably disconnected themselves. I often see many of these, and in different networks with different OSes and different configurations (without fail2ban or equivalent), so the reason is at the zombie end, not at the MX end. In the CONNECT case, most likely the reverse DNS lookup took longer than the zombie could wait (maybe zombie authoris prefer servers that don't do any checks, including rDNS lookup, as this means better deliverability for their junk?). Other possibilities include the zombie crash/reboot/disconnected/... etc. > > # postconf -n > address_verify_map = btree:/var/spool/postfix/etc/verify > alias_database = hash:/etc/aliases > alias_maps = hash:/etc/aliases > allow_percent_hack = no > anvil_rate_time_unit = 60s > anvil_status_update_time = 1d > append_dot_mydomain = yes > biff = no > body_checks = pcre:/etc/postfix/body_checks.pcre > broken_sasl_auth_clients = yes > config_directory = /etc/postfix > daemon_timeout = 2d > delay_warning_time = 4h > disable_vrfy_command = yes > header_checks = pcre:/etc/postfix/header_checks.pcre > html_directory = /usr/share/doc/postfix/html > inet_interfaces = all > mailbox_command = procmail -a "$EXTENSION" > mailbox_size_limit = 0 > message_size_limit = 0 > mime_header_checks = $header_checks > mydestination = $myhostname, localhost.$mydomain, localhost, $mydomain > mydomain = localdomain > myhostname = > mynetworks = 127.0.0.0/8 192.168.0.0/24 > myorigin = $myhostname > nested_header_checks = $header_checks > readme_directory = /usr/share/doc/postfix > recipient_delimiter = + > relayhost = show_user_unknown_table_name = no > smtp_bind_address = > smtpd_banner = $myhostname remove this. the default is good. > smtpd_client_restrictions = smtpd_data_restrictions = > reject_unauth_pipelining ahuh? this should be smtpd_client_restrictions= smtpd_data_restrictions = reject_unauth_pipelining you have a leading whitespace before your smtpd_data_restrictions. remove it. > smtpd_delay_reject = yes > smtpd_discard_ehlo_keyword_address_maps = > cidr:/etc/postfix/ehlo_keyword_map.cidr > smtpd_end_of_data_restrictions = reject_unauth_pipelining > smtpd_etrn_restrictions = permit_mynetworks, reject > smtpd_helo_required = yes > smtpd_helo_restrictions = smtpd_policy_service_max_idle = 600s > smtpd_policy_service_timeout = 600s > smtpd_recipient_restrictions = permitlocal, relaycheck, > presenderchecks, prependheader, prewhitelists, > stndchecks, whitelists, pcrechecks, > cidrchecks, hashchecks, unlistchecks, > spfcheck, rhsblchecks, policydcheck, > greylist > smtpd_restriction_classes = cidrchecks greylist > hashchecks pcrechecks permitlocal > policydcheck prependheader presenderchecks > prewhitelists rblchecks relaycheck > rhsblchecks spamchecks spfcheck > stndchecks testblists unlistchecks > verifysender whitelists unofrtunately, postconf -n does not show restrictions classes (what checks they contain), so we have no idea what checks you are actually doing. > smtpd_sasl_auth_enable = yes > smtpd_sasl_local_domain = $mydomain > smtpd_sasl_security_options = noanonymous > smtpd_sender_restrictions = soft_bounce = no > strict_rfc821_envelopes = yes > swap_bangpath = no > unverified_sender_reject_code = 550 > > Justin.

I often see many of these, and in different networks with different OSes and different configurations (without fail2ban or equivalent), so the reason is at the zombie end, not at the MX end. In the CONNECT case, most likely the reverse DNS lookup took longer than the zombie could wait (maybe zombie authoris prefer servers that don't do any checks, including rDNS lookup, as this means better deliverability for their junk?). Other possibilities include the zombie crash/reboot/disconnected/... etc. > > # postconf -n > address_verify_map = btree:/var/spool/postfix/etc/verify > alias_database = hash:/etc/aliases > alias_maps = hash:/etc/aliases > allow_percent_hack = no > anvil_rate_time_unit = 60s > anvil_status_update_time = 1d > append_dot_mydomain = yes > biff = no > body_checks = pcre:/etc/postfix/body_checks.pcre > broken_sasl_auth_clients = yes > config_directory = /etc/postfix > daemon_timeout = 2d > delay_warning_time = 4h > disable_vrfy_command = yes > header_checks = pcre:/etc/postfix/header_checks.pcre > html_directory = /usr/share/doc/postfix/html > inet_interfaces = all > mailbox_command = procmail -a "$EXTENSION" > mailbox_size_limit = 0 > message_size_limit = 0 > mime_header_checks = $header_checks > mydestination = $myhostname, localhost.$mydomain, localhost, $mydomain > mydomain = localdomain > myhostname = > mynetworks = 127.0.0.0/8 192.168.0.0/24 > myorigin = $myhostname > nested_header_checks = $header_checks > readme_directory = /usr/share/doc/postfix > recipient_delimiter = + > relayhost = show_user_unknown_table_name = no > smtp_bind_address = > smtpd_banner = $myhostname remove this. the default is good. > smtpd_client_restrictions = smtpd_data_restrictions = > reject_unauth_pipelining ahuh? this should be smtpd_client_restrictions= smtpd_data_restrictions = reject_unauth_pipelining you have a leading whitespace before your smtpd_data_restrictions. remove it. > smtpd_delay_reject = yes > smtpd_discard_ehlo_keyword_address_maps = > cidr:/etc/postfix/ehlo_keyword_map.cidr > smtpd_end_of_data_restrictions = reject_unauth_pipelining > smtpd_etrn_restrictions = permit_mynetworks, reject > smtpd_helo_required = yes > smtpd_helo_restrictions = smtpd_policy_service_max_idle = 600s > smtpd_policy_service_timeout = 600s > smtpd_recipient_restrictions = permitlocal, relaycheck, > presenderchecks, prependheader, prewhitelists, > stndchecks, whitelists, pcrechecks, > cidrchecks, hashchecks, unlistchecks, > spfcheck, rhsblchecks, policydcheck, > greylist > smtpd_restriction_classes = cidrchecks greylist > hashchecks pcrechecks permitlocal > policydcheck prependheader presenderchecks > prewhitelists rblchecks relaycheck > rhsblchecks spamchecks spfcheck > stndchecks testblists unlistchecks > verifysender whitelists unofrtunately, postconf -n does not show restrictions classes (what checks they contain), so we have no idea what checks you are actually doing. > smtpd_sasl_auth_enable = yes > smtpd_sasl_local_domain = $mydomain > smtpd_sasl_security_options = noanonymous > smtpd_sender_restrictions = soft_bounce = no > strict_rfc821_envelopes = yes > swap_bangpath = no > unverified_sender_reject_code = 550 > > Justin.

Subject: 2025 VinFuture Prize Nomination Deadline вАУ April 17 (Vietnam time GMT+7)
Body: To view this email as a web page, click here. Hanoi, April 8, 2025 Dear Dr. Hansen: We hope this message finds you well. With just 9 days remaining until the deadline for the 2025 VinFuture Prize Nomination Submission and Nominator Recognition Program, we kindly encourage you, as a valued member of the global scientific community, to submit nominations for outstanding scientific and technological innovations that create a positive and meaningful impact on humanity. The VinFuture Prize is the core activity of the VinFuture Foundation, a non-profit organization established in Vietnam on International Human Solidarity Day on December 20, 2020. The VinFuture Prize aims to recognize transformative scientific and technological innovations capable of making significant positive changes in the lives of millions of people worldwide. If you have already submitted a nomination, we sincerely appreciate your contribution and are happy to assist with any updates or additional submissions. If you are still considering nominating, we kindly encourage you to do so before the April 17, 2025 deadline. For submission guidelines and further details, please visit https://online.vinfutureprize.org/nomination/. Should you have any questions, feel free to contact us at secretariat@vinfutureprize.org. Thank you for your time and support in recognizing transformative innovations. Best regards, VinFuture Prize Management & Secretariat [T] (+84)-024-3208-5588 (Ext. 4079) (9:00 AMвАУ5:00 PM, GMT +7) [E] secretariat@vinfutureprize.org [W] https://vinfutureprize.org/ [FB] https://www.facebook.com/vinfutureprize [A] Vinhomes Ocean Park, Gia Lam District, Ha Noi, Viet Nam Update Profile Unsubscribe

Subject: Re: clarification of our inclusion policy (fwd)
Body: Michael Peddemors writes: > And with the current SA, this is more difficult to turn off via a config > option for example.. I do not think it is difficult. Does setting the scores of the following rules to zero in local.cf not turn off spamhaus RBL checking? __RCVD_IN_ZEN RCVD_IN_SBL RCVD_IN_XBL RCVD_IN_PBL I have set these in my systems (as they reject on zen at SMTP time) and I do not think that Spamassassin querying spamhaus since I added these rules.

Subject: Re: [opensuse] -noarch.rpm
Body: On Sunday 10 February 2008 23:32:31 Stan Goodman wrote: > I want to install FAX4CUPS to work with HylaFax, and have found a package > for it that is a noarch.rpm. My understanding from definitions I find on > the Web is that this means it is CPU-hardware independent, so I can use > it in a x86_64 system; what about distribution dependence?. The package > is not marked as being associated with any distribution; can I use it > with openSuSE? You can use it with openSUSE, but unfortunately the package installs to /usr/lib/cups/backend. On a 64 bit system the correct directory is /usr/lib64/cups/backend It's just shell scripts, so they should work. You just have to move them from /usr/lib to /usr/lib64 Anders -- Madness takes its toll
text:
Re: [opensuse] -noarch.rpm

On Sunday 10 February 2008 23:32:31 Stan Goodman wrote: > I want to install FAX4CUPS to work with HylaFax, and have found a package > for it that is a noarch.rpm. My understanding from definitions I find on > the Web is that this means it is CPU-hardware independent, so I can use > it in a x86_64 system; what about distribution dependence?. The package > is not marked as being associated with any distribution; can I use it > with openSuSE? You can use it with openSUSE, but unfortunately the package installs to /usr/lib/cups/backend. On a 64 bit system the correct directory is /usr/lib64/cups/backend It's just shell scripts, so they should work. You just have to move them from /usr/lib to /usr/lib64 Anders -- Madness takes its toll
